era_context: Iron Age II. Shalmaneser III's reign continues in Assyria. The Levant remains under pressure from Assyrian expansion. Israel, Aram-Damascus, and Phoenician city-states navigate the complex politics of the region. Egypt is fragmented. This is a period of relative stability in the Levant before the major upheavals of the late 9th century BCE.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Most regions outside the Assyrian sphere of influence

events.title: Shalmaneser III's 8th regnal year campaign
events.description: Shalmaneser III's 8th regnal year campaign (c. 851 or 822 BCE depending on chronology) continues the pattern of Assyrian military expansion into the Levant. The campaign targets Syrian and Palestinian states, reinforcing Assyrian hegemony. The specific targets and outcomes of this campaign are recorded in Assyrian annals, though details vary depending on which inscription is consulted.
events.certainty_note: The assignment of this campaign to 822 BCE is based on chronological reconstruction and is uncertain by ±5 years. Shalmaneser's regnal years are well-documented, but the absolute dating depends on which chronological system is adopted.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are well-documented for 822 BCE. The assignment of Shalmaneser's campaigns to specific years is inherently uncertain and depends on chronological reconstruction. Different scholarly chronologies place the same campaigns in different years.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 822 BCE is very sparse. No specific events are well-documented for this year. The only source material comes from Assyrian annals, which are focused on military campaigns and do not provide detailed information about other regions or internal developments. The lack of specific documentation for this year reflects the general sparsity of written records for most of the ancient world outside the Assyrian and Egyptian spheres.
